comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: STATE: OPEN, NOT built — ESCALATED. HMAC scheme removal (contract phase) + expand-contract migration doc (docs/migrations/hmac-opcert-removal.md). BLOCKED on a design fork coupled to ee0b: removing HMAC requires the gate PRODUCERS to mint asymmetric op-certs, which needs the producer-key decision — (A) every environment (incl local dev) holds its own Ed25519 op-cert key and signs locally, vs (B) only the trusted rebar-mcp server (ee0b) signs and local runs produce unsigned records. This intersects ee0b's double-signing question (review_plan self-signs HMAC today). Must be designed with ee0b. Description carries the expand/contract artifact spec.

compiled_state.description:
# op-cert: remove the legacy HMAC scheme (contract phase) via expand-contract migration

## Why
Once the producer signing seam ships (the expand phase: producers mint Ed25519 op-certs, the read
path accepts both envelopes and legacy HMAC records), the HMAC scheme is dead weight for op-cert
kinds — and leaving it verifiable keeps a forgeable path alive (anyone holding a clone's symmetric
secret can mint a "certified" verdict). This contract phase removes HMAC signing AND verification
for the op-cert kinds (`plan-review`, `completion-verifier`), completing the migration begun in
the foundation epic. The producer-key fork this story previously carried is settled in the epic's
"Producer-signing & compute-runtime design resolution" (fork A: every environment signs locally
with its own Ed25519 key) — this story only removes the legacy path.

**Hard dependency (not yet on `main`):** the producer signing seam story (the expand phase) and
the op-cert stack (Gerrit changes 649/650/660, in review) must land first — the asymmetric
producer path this story contracts onto does not exist on current `origin/main`; enforced by the
`depends_on` link and the dependency-gate AC below.

## What
- Remove the HMAC registration for the op-cert kinds from `src/rebar/attest/hmac_legacy.py` /
  `attest/registry.py` `POLICY` so neither `plan-review` nor `completion-verifier` resolves the
  `HMAC-SHA256` scheme. Shared functions are enumerated explicitly — changed vs untouched:
  - **changed**: `signing.sign_manifest` (the expand phase's dual-shape window closes: the HMAC
    branch for op-cert kinds is deleted, envelope-only); `hmac_legacy` op-cert kind
    registrations (deleted); the op-cert read path (HMAC records for these kinds no longer
    verify); `sign_result.schema.json` tightened to envelope-only for these kinds.
  - **untouched**: `signing.compute_signature` (generic HMAC utility), the `.signing-key`
    genesis and its non-op-cert consumers (`rebar init` seeds it; generic manifest verify), and
    the authorship kind. `.signing-key` removal is explicitly OUT of scope.
- Read path post-contract: a pre-existing HMAC attestation reads as **not-certified** (validity is
  computed on read) — it is preserved in the event log, never rewritten. Re-issue = re-run the
  gate (`rebar review-plan` re-signs the current plan; re-closing re-earns the completion cert).
- MCP surface: `sign_manifest` / `verify_signature` operate exclusively on the op-cert seam for
  these kinds; their docs/help updated.
- **Migration artifact** `docs/migrations/hmac-opcert-removal.md` (a named observable artifact):
  `## Expand` (the producer-seam story: write-new/read-both) and `## Contract` (this story)
  sections, the cutover procedure (upgrade order: reconcile hosts first, per the store rollout
  rule — older clones preserve-and-ignore envelope events and `fsck` WARNs), the re-issue
  procedure for still-live HMAC attestations, and the rollback posture (required-environment
  policy is opt-in and defaults off; absent `.rebar/trusted_environments.yaml` /
  `verify.require_environment` unset keeps low-security behavior).
- No flag-day break: tickets whose HMAC attestation goes stale simply re-earn certs on their next
  gate run; closed history is untouched.

## Scope
- In: HMAC removal for op-cert kinds, read-path contract behavior, the migration doc, MCP-surface
  updates, tests below.
- Out: the authorship kind (already asymmetric, identity epic); the DSSE/SSHSIG substrate; the
  trusted service (ee0b) and its IaC; any trusted-env policy change.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dependency gate: the asymmetric producer path is verified present before any removal — an
      import check plus an end-to-end op-cert sign/verify smoke test pass on the branch this
      story builds on; recorded as the story's first verification step.
- [ ] A test asserts no code path signs an HMAC operation-cert: the `plan-review` /
      `completion-verifier` kinds no longer resolve the `HMAC-SHA256` scheme in
      `registry.POLICY`, and `signing.sign_manifest` has no HMAC branch reachable for these
      kinds (`compute_signature` and `.signing-key` remain for non-op-cert consumers).
- [ ] A test asserts no code path ACCEPTS an HMAC operation-cert: a pre-existing HMAC-signed
      attestation for either kind reads as not-certified post-contract (preserved in the log,
      never rewritten), and re-running the gate re-issues a valid Ed25519 op-cert on the same
      ticket.
- [ ] `docs/migrations/hmac-opcert-removal.md` exists, is non-empty, and contains `## Expand` and
      `## Contract` sections plus the cutover (upgrade-order) and re-issue procedures; a test
      asserts existence, non-emptiness, and both section headings.
- [ ] The authorship kind (`rebar.authorship.v1`) and any non-op-cert HMAC consumer are untouched;
      a test asserts authorship verification still passes post-removal.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` green.

file_impact.reason: delete HMAC sign/verify path for op-certs

compiled_state.title: op-cert: remove legacy HMAC scheme (contract phase) + expand-contract migration
